TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The P.s. Path – Part 2
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
Key Takeaways:
• 	This lesson covers the advanced portion of PS Path
• 	If you recall the classic “Problem-Agitate-Solve”, remember that some agitation is
still required
• 	You always have to show the downside – what will happen if they don’t buy your
product
• 	In most classic “Hollywood” stories, there is a moment where they hit rock bottom.
Eventually, they come back up. There’s a clear beginning, middle, and end
• 	Once you define the problem it’s important to agitate. There is always one grand
meta problem-solution. Within that there are mini problem-solutions that you’ll
solve and weave into the grander story
• 	The smaller problems are educating the prospect on what their problems are, i.e.
what they don’t know or are ignorant about. e.g. people don’t know about the many
different types of problems
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT:
Here’s part two. The advanced part of the PS Path.
There are a couple of subtleties here. Remember that old Problem, Agitate,
Solve? A lot of people don’t like the agitate part. They don’t like that ‘stick of the knife
in the wound’ type of thing because we want to save the world, provide value and sell.
You still need to do some of the agitation. You absolutely have to.
Think about Hollywood, your typical movie. Here you’ve got your romantic
comedy... Boy meets girl and then they are either going to end up happy and married,
or else they’re going to end up happy with someone else. You have this romantic
comedy. Let’s just say that the male is the protagonist and he’s in this relationship.
He’s going to end up in a relationship either with this other person, or someone
different.
There are going to be all kinds of trials and tribulations, adventure and
comedy in between. What always happens is that they just got from here to here.
What happens is they go to rock bottom. They lose their job, their dog, their house.
They walk around in their bathrobe, crying walking down the street for four weeks.
They hit rock bottom and that’s the story and then they come back up.
If you look at any movie coming out of Hollywood, it’s like that. People don’t
start at one level and move higher, or they don’t start at one level and end at that
level.
Look at The Lord of the Rings. They go through absolute hell here. That’s
what pulls people in. That’s what story is all about. There was this beginning, and
then there’s this end. It’s usually happy or big or whatever, but in between they take
you down. They hit rock bottom.
When you define the problem, you do have to agitate. You do have to
“dimensionalize.” That’s a copywriting term.
When we’re talking about Product Launch Formula, the big problem is that
you need to launch, but it’s not that I just come out and say, “You need to launch.” I
say, “You need to launch because every great product, brand or business starts with a
great launch. If you don’t launch you are going to go out of business, or your business
is going to shut down. You’re going to put all of this time and effort in and it will be
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

4
TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The P.s. Path – Part 2
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
all lost. You’re going to be going back to working at Walmart, or wherever.”
I don’t actually say that, but the thing is I don’t just say, “You have to launch.”
I say, “You have to launch because you will fail if you don’t.” There is some amount of
agitation there. You always have to show the down side. That’s one distinction.
The other distinction is that—remember in the old style the Problem, Agitate,
Solve on this sales path, there’s basically one problem and one solution? Here we’re
breaking it up and we’re delivering value by defining sub-problems, smaller problems
and then we’re giving the solutions in there, but there’s still one meta-story over it
all.
The story, like with Product Launch Formula, is you need to launch and the
solution is you buy PLF. You actually do a PLF launch. The problem is you need to
launch. The solution is you need to get PLF and do a launch. That meta-story is all
weaved in throughout all these pieces, but then within them we’re breaking it down.
Now you need to do the sideways sales letter. You need to know what type of
launch to do. You need to know the product launch blueprint. We’re still breaking it
down and giving solutions all the way through here, but you are carrying that big
picture Problem Solution Path throughout the entire thing.
What you're doing in each one of these is with this problem, with this
problem, with this problem you’re really educating them on what their problems are.
It’s really powerful. People don’t even know that they need a sideways sales letter
before they wonder into my world. Then I show them you do need the sideways sales
letter. I’m educating them on their ignorance basically.
I know ignorance is an awful word. It’s something that people don’t know. So
I’m educating them. Here’s we’re educating them on the different kinds of launches.
People didn’t know that there were all these different types of launches. I’m
educating them and giving them a solution. Each step of the way, you’re providing
more education. A lot of times the education is about what they don’t know. Now
you’re solving it and you’re giving them the solution.
That’s the couple of things. First you do have to take people down. You do
have to do some of that agitation, and you have one big meta-path. Within that big
path, you’re adding in these different layers.
The Problem Solution Path - it works and it’s how you deliver
great value and make a sale.
